title: lichen_lora_l2_tx takes non-const pointer implying buffer may be modified
description:
Location: lora_l2.h:130-141, lora_l2.c:431

**What goes wrong:** The function signature is `int lichen_lora_l2_tx(uint8_t *data, size_t len)` with a comment warning that the buffer may be modified by DMA or in-place encryption. However, lichen_l2_send() at line 273 passes tx_frame_buf which is static. If lora_send() modifies the buffer, the next TX might start with corrupted data if frame building reuses portions.

Actually, looking at lichen_l2_send: lichen_link_tx() builds a fresh frame into tx_frame_buf each time, overwriting any previous contents. So even if lora_send() modifies it, the next TX rebuilds from scratch.

Not a bug in current code. But the API contract is a footgun for future callers who might pass a buffer they expect to remain unchanged.

This is more of a documentation issue / API design concern. Worth noting but P1 not P0.
